text:
Once upon a time, in a town overlooked by eagles and forgotten by historians, there lived a boy named Jamie. Jamie had a vibrant imagination that saw landscapes far beyond the dusty yellow bricks of his hometown. One sunny summer morning, while his family was running errands, Jamie gathered his slippers, his nose pinched in a tissue, and skipped to the edge of the wooded lot behind their house.

He found a secret patch he claimed as his own—a clearing with mossy ground and a small, enchanted spring. It was in this hidden gem that Jamie discovered the Lost Toy of Whispering Wood, a shiny, antique-looking clock without hands or numbers, but teetering precariously atop a cobblestone circle. The clock seemed to hum with a curious tune.

He decided to name it "The Time Whisperer." Each night, Jamie would put it by his pillow and listen to its melodious chimes. The clock talked to him—a stream of memories of towns he’d
